title: ImageFactory
description: AssetFactory implementation that loads PNG, JPG, WebP, AVIF, and other browser-supported raster image formats and produces a decoded image. When `createImageBitmap` is available (all modern browsers) the factory returns an ImageBitmap — a zero-copy GPU-upload path that avoids the HTMLImageElement render-pipeline overhead. On older environments it falls back to an HTMLImageElement. MIME type detection is performed automatically from the buffer's magic bytes; pass an explicit `mimeType` in options to override.

sections.title: Import
paragraphs: AssetFactory implementation that loads PNG, JPG, WebP, AVIF, and other browser-supported raster image formats and produces a decoded image.

When `createImageBitmap` is available (all modern browsers) the factory returns an ImageBitmap — a zero-copy GPU-upload path that avoids the HTMLImageElement render-pipeline overhead. On older environments it falls back to an HTMLImageElement.
MIME type detection is performed automatically from the buffer's magic bytes; pass an explicit `mimeType` in options to override.

members.name: create
members.description: Decodes image bytes into a DecodedImage. Prefers createImageBitmap for a zero-copy GPU-upload path. Falls back to a temporary object URL + element on environments that do not support createImageBitmap.

members.name: createObjectUrl
members.description: Creates an object URL from blob and registers it for automatic cleanup when destroy is called. Prefer this over calling URL.createObjectURL directly inside a factory subclass so the URL is always tracked.

members.name: destroy
members.description: Revokes every object URL that was created via createObjectUrl and clears the internal tracking pool.

members.name: process
members.description: Reads the full response body as an ArrayBuffer for MIME-type detection and blob construction.

members.name: storageName
members.description: Identifier used as the object-store / storage-namespace key when this factory's assets are persisted to a CacheStore.
